pattern: Runs of equal values collapse to a single logical position, so squeezing consecutive duplicates down to one representative per run turns hill/valley detection into a simple compare-with-neighbor pass.
hints: A run of equal values all share the same hill-or-valley status, so first compress the array down to one entry per maximal run of equal values.

Once duplicates are compressed, every remaining interior position (not the first or last) is either a hill, a valley, or neither, purely by comparing it to its immediate left and right neighbor in the compressed sequence.
Walk the compressed array from the second to the second-to-last position, and count a hit whenever the value is strictly greater than both neighbors or strictly less than both neighbors.

edge_inputs.description: one hill and one valley with a shared-run valley in the middle
monotonic zigzag with no equal runs at all
minimum length, strictly increasing so neither endpoint qualifies and there is no interior
the very first and last elements can never be a hill or valley even if locally extreme
a long flat run in the middle counts as a single hill despite spanning many indices
the whole array is one constant run, so there are no non-equal neighbors anywhere
two adjacent hills separated by a single dip that itself is too shallow to be a valley

stress.description: 100-element array with heavy repetition to stress the run-compression logic
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 8) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: small value range over a length-100 array forces frequent equal-value runs
